reviews.review_title: BUYER BEWARE - UPDATES HAVE BROKEN GAME
reviews.review_description: This game used to be good, but the latest updates have ruined much of the experience. It feels like this game has been bought by some outside private equity company and reduced to a shadow of its former self. They've removed the ability to have custom avatars, or even to use your own Meta avatar like you could with the previous version of the game (although the bot/shill developer response to these reviews loves to be deliberately obtuse and pretend that it's still a feature. It isn't. But I bet they're "super bummed" you didn't have the best experience....instead of...y'know...fixing what they broke in the first place). And the game is now generally a buggy mess where half the features--including multiplayer--don't work correctly. It is no longer worth the price tag. Buyer beware.
This game does not work anymore hardly, stuck in loading screen constantly, cannot find players to be matched with. Unless you want to play by yourself and have loading screen freezes do not buy.

Game fun but no players to play with. I would recommend if you like single player games. A big variety of balls to chose from
.

I just got a Quest. And I mostly got it for exercise and experience type gaming to share with the family. Reminiscent of the Wii days. So this game was top of my list for some family fun gaming. I love the pass and play feature and think that more games should implement this because not everyone can afford more than one headset let alone one. So this is great for that! You can choose how many frames you each play before swapping so that you're not constantly back and forth and it is pretty intuitive. Even my mum is able to get to grips with it as she misses the Wii days.
There is a cool variety of balls with different styles and applications to them, so that provides a fun addition. It's great that there are plenty to unlock without a paywall. Live-service methods in gaming are so toxic, so having decent unlockables is nice to see. The world is vibrant and immersive in a simple yet accessible way. The bowling feels decent and you're able to fully apply a swing with fluid movement. I haven't known whether you can use your hands instead of controllers yet though but if you can then that would be cool to try!

One annoyance though is the bowl-assist modes. Maybe it told me at the start but I didn't realise about this full aim-assist type of mechanic and me and my family were quite disheartened when we found out we weren't doing as good as we thought we were. I felt bad that I was winning by a lot so I purposefully "fumbled" my next bowl right into the gutter and got a strike. It would not go in the gutter at all no matter how hard I tried.
I was glad of the different options for "aim-assist" and experimented. I went to Classic, I believe, with no assistance. That is a more rewarding and challenging way to play it because the full aim-assist was pointless and hollow. The non-assisted style is taking some getting used to but we're all feeling that this is down to skill and perseverance rather than cheating in any way. Which is good because games are more dynamic and down to practise.
I suggest that everyone puts it on classic throw style! 
It's not a perfect game but it runs well for the most part! Great to get to grips (no pun intended) with VR and a fun casual game time with family of most age ranges. It's not quite real bowling but then it shouldn't have to be, it just needs to be close enough and this game nails that! Reminiscent of the old Wii days but to the next level, and that is something we need now in gaming. Pass and play is great! There isn't enough of that! Please keep making more of this type of thing! Keep working at it and improving, we need this sort of gaming! And we need to support it as well.
Blabber review over.
Thank you to the devs and to anyone that takes the time to read this...sorry but I hope my sincere blabber helps.

This was the best game ever especially with the speed you can me personally I got 38.5 with whipping my arm so fast it doesn't respond to the game . But sometimes it hit sk hard it faces through the pins and that needs fixed please but 4 stars recommend to all who love bowling.

BY FAR the BEST bowling game in VR. The bowling balls are amazing looking, dozens to choose from, perhaps could use a few more different alleys (maybe an old 80s tournament style) but its just so fun. The AR version is INCREDIBLE.  You literally have a bowling lane in your room. The depth perception is perfect....extremely immersive.

This game use to be the best out there there just bugs in game itll take nearly 15 minutes just to get in before you even start playing and it's the same with forevr darts as well it takes 15 minutes as well to load in I'm having to go on to menu while its loading and press continue countless times before it loads you in its a massive issue

Was my absolute favorite of all the games; it will be forever missed. I was able to move around, had options for balls, and the best feature was playing the game with music from your YouTube library. 

Please bring forever bowl back; because it’s not worth $.3

reviews.review_title: Only forevr title missing mixed reality
reviews.review_description:
Really hope the game dev can update this game to include some sort of m.r. portal that appears in the wall and mixed reality so that the bowling alley basically feels like it's in the wall.

Maybe we can set Lane dimensions, and just place them anywhere on the floor. However the portal option probably would be the easiest, Mixed reality filter.

developer_response.body:
Hello PainTitan, ForeVR Bowl does have mixed reality. Are you having trouble accessing it from within the pause menu? Please reach out to our support if you need assistance. 
(link: https://forevrgames.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/requests/new)
Thank you! We hope you enjoy the MR mode as much as we do! Team ForeVR

reviews.review_description: You took away pass and play which is what buyers paid for so so based on that i would call it fraud - you should be ashamed of yourself and so should meta for allowing you to do it - YOU broke the product so YOU should refund the hard earned money spent

developer_response.body: Hello bobbywins22, Pass And Play is still a feature in the game with a reduced party size of two players. We will be sharing your thoughts with the rest of the team. - Team ForeVR

reviews.review_title: Good unless you want to play with others
reviews.review_description: This (and the other forevr) games used to be a lot fun until recently when developers took away the multiplayer feature that allowed players to find and join up to 3 other players currently playing (for a total of 4). No real explanation as to why they did it either. Now you have this “Quick Match” feature that’s limited to only 1v1 (vs up to 4 players before) and because of this you rarely find anyone to play with. Not only that, but bowling doesn’t even have an AI to play against so you’re literally playing by yourself. There are avatars in the alley but they are not playing and you can’t play any of them so it’s you against yourself. Very very boring! You can play up to 4 players IF you coordinate with friends you already know that can join you at a specific time but. So if you’re looking for a fun social gaming experience where you can meet/find other players online to play with then unfortunately this isn’t for you.

developer_response.body:
Hello netsurfr1, We are bummed to hear about your experience. We made multiplayer changes to help our players find opponents more quickly and seamlessly, we’ve improved the public multiplayer matchmaking experience. Quick Match now pairs two players (instead of four) to ensure players find an opponent. We will be discussing your multiplayer experience and feedback with the rest of the team. 
If you have any other feedback player send it to us here (link: https://forevrgames.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/requests/new) - Team ForeVR

reviews.review_description: As a single player game it's amazing as a multilayer game it's awful no opponents to play waits for a opponents over 25 minutes

developer_response.body:
Hello Neon147, thank you for sharing your experience with us and for picking up ForeVR Bowling! If you are ever looking for others to play with a lot of our players have had success by joining our discord! If you haven't already we'd love for you to join, (link: https://discord.gg/forevrgames)
If you have any other feedback you'd like to share with us please reach out to us here (link: https://forevrgames.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/requests/new) We want you to have the best time playing our games! 
- Team ForeVR

reviews.review_description: I love bowling in reality, and I have to keep in mind that this is really a game. However, limited space and choice of balls is intimidating. I'm fairly new in this game, so patience is key.

developer_response.body: Hi Char1055! Thanks for your review! We would LOVE to help recommend some bowling balls but we like to know what your throw style is, could you drop us an email at support@forevrgames.com so we can talk? Thanks! Some of our personal favorites are the Dark Mage, Earth, Glitter Patrol, and The Ancient! We look forward to talking with you! Team ForeVR

reviews.review_description:
So we just started to play this today..total newb….I like the game but noticed after I roll a few times,  I end up on a different lane and can’t get back to where the ball are lol…then I’m asked to go to the bowling store and I can figure out how to get back to the lanes…..
Guess I just need to practice more lol but liking the game

developer_response.body:
Hey rbh55! Thank you for your review and feedback! You can hold the Oculus button to recenter to the front of the lane. All party starting and getting into a lane can be done by interacting with the bowling pin shaped computer from within the lobby! If you need further assistance or have any other questions please feel free to reach out to us here: forevrgames.com/support
Game on! Team ForeVR

reviews.review_description: Bought and can’t figure out how to play without buying credits. This sucks

developer_response.body: Hi Nate! Is it possible you might be confusing ForeVR Bowl with a different game? We don't offer any in-game purchases, just the base game. To unlock levels and earn currency to buy more balls, you just need to play a few games to level up. You start with access to multiple halls plus if you’re looking for more balls to add to your arsenal, try looking for a few hidden ones to unlock (there are even a few in the lobby!) Let us know if you still need help! forevrgames.com/support

reviews.review_description: Having a great time with this game

developer_response.body: Hey Noles1981! Glad you're having a great time!🎳 Game on! Team ForeVR
